description: 'norawhi' is the fading light after or around sunset as night approaches. It is Phi's evening threshold and pairs with 'horathe' (dawn), the first light at the other side of night.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n' and carry nasal airflow into middle-height rounded 'o'. Tap or briefly trill the tongue and open into stressed central 'a'. Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for unvoiced 'wh', then finish with high-front unrounded 'i'.

semantic_domains.nature: the final fading of daylight around sunset
semantic_domains.temporal: the threshold station between evening and night
